digest:
Ensemble is a library-first TypeScript toolkit for centrally managing Claude Code extension artifacts across AI clients. The library at `~/.config/ensemble/library/` is the authoritative inventory of seven resource types—MCP servers, skills, plugins, subagents, slash commands, hooks, and managed settings—each carrying dual annotations (source-owned `description` and user-authored `userNotes`). Install state is a per-client/per-project property of library resources, not a separate tier. Operations are pure functions using Zod schemas, and sync projects this state to clients via additive writes, non-destructive merges for settings and hooks, and symlink fan-out for file-based resources. The platform adopts a "Claude-Code-first, multi-client second" stance, managing all seven types end-to-end for Claude Code before expanding to other clients.

The platform supports 17 AI clients (Claude Desktop, Code, Cursor, VS Code, Windsurf, Zed, JetBrains, etc.) with 4 planned. Sync strategies vary by type: servers and plugins use config-entry writes; skills, agents, and commands use symlink fan-out from a canonical store; hooks and settings merge non-destructively into `settings.json`. Every sync run produces a rollback-capable snapshot. Drift detection tracks local content hash changes and upstream tree hash changes (for GitHub sources). The desktop app provides a pivot-based sidebar (Library, By Project, By Group, By Client, Marketplace) and a project × Claude Code matrix view for wiring resources. Features include typed variables with inheritance, profile-as-plugin packaging, and read-only integration with the project registry and setlist capability registry.

The CLI uses Commander.js with a v1.3-style verb surface (e.g., `ensemble plugins install`) while the v2.0.1 lifecycle verbs (`pull`, `add`, `install`, `uninstall`, `remove`) and library subcommands are unbuilt. The Electron desktop app uses React + Tailwind with a tRPC bridge. Unbuilt work includes the `ensemble browse` discovery engine, the `ensemble settings` group wiring, and the `ensemble import-legacy` throwaway transitional command. The `ensemble doctor` command performs deterministic health audits. The `ensemble init` command provides guided onboarding. The `ensemble registry` module supports the Official MCP Registry and Glama.
